2605.14937.title: Slot-MPC: Goal-Conditioned Model Predictive Control with Object-Centric Representations
2605.14937.paper_summary: Predictive world models enable agents to model scene dynamics and reason about the consequences of their actions. Inspired by human perception, object-centric world models capture scene dynamics using object-level representations, which can be used for downstream applications such as action planning. However, most object-centric world models and reinforcement learning (RL) approaches learn reactive policies that are fixed at inference time, limiting generalization to novel situations. We propose Slot-MPC, an object-centric world modeling framework that enables planning through Model Predictive Control (MPC). Slot-MPC leverages vision encoders to learn slot-based representations, which encode individual objects in the scene, and uses these structured representations to learn an action-conditioned object-centric dynamics model. At inference time, the learned dynamics model enables action planning via MPC, allowing agents to adapt to previously unseen situations. Since the learned world model is differentiable, we can use gradient-based MPC to directly optimize actions, which is computationally more efficient than relying on gradient-free, sampling-based MPC methods. Experiments on simulated robotic manipulation tasks show that Slot-MPC improves both task performance and planning efficiency compared to non-object-centric world model baselines. In the considered offline setting with limited state-action coverage, we find that gradient-based MPC performs better than gradient-free, sampling-based MPC. Our results demonstrate that explicitly structured, object-centric representations provide a strong inductive bias for controllable and generalizable decision-making. Code and additional results are available at https://slot-mpc.github.io.
2605.14937.paper_summary_zh: 預測世界模型使代理能夠建模場景動態並推理其行動的後果。受到人類感知的啟發，以物體為中心的世界模型使用物體級別的表示來捕捉場景動態，這些表示可以用於下游應用，如行動規劃。然而，大多數以物體為中心的世界模型和強化學習（RL）方法學習的是在推理時固定的反應性策略，這限制了對新情況的泛化。我們提出了Slot-MPC，一個以物體為中心的世界建模框架，通過模型預測控制（MPC）來實現規劃。Slot-MPC利用視覺編碼器學習基於插槽的表示，這些表示編碼場景中的個別物體，並使用這些結構化表示來學習一個以行動為條件的物體中心動態模型。在推理時，學習到的動態模型通過MPC實現行動規劃，使代理能夠適應先前未見過的情況。由於學習到的世界模型是可微分的，我們可以使用基於梯度的MPC直接優化行動，這在計算上比依賴於無梯度的、基於採樣的MPC方法更有效。對模擬機器人操作任務的實驗表明，與非物體中心的世界模型基準相比，Slot-MPC在任務性能和規劃效率上都有所改善。在考慮到有限狀態-行動覆蓋的離線設置中，我們發現基於梯度的MPC表現優於無梯度的、基於採樣的MPC。我們的結果表明，明確結構化的以物體為中心的表示為可控和可泛化的決策提供了強大的歸納偏差。代碼和其他結果可在 https://slot-mpc.github.io 獲得。
2605.14937.author: Jonathan Spieler et.al.
2605.14937.authors: Jonathan Spieler, Angel Villar-Corrales, Sven Behnke
